message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Code ready for review — Step 4 complete, CI green">
Step 4 (Next Actions with Contexts) implementation is complete and CI is green. Luca has pushed the code and it's ready for review. Please review the changes from your area of expertise and send your feedback to luca-palmieri. Remember to categorize as BLOCKING, Minor, or Nice-to-have, and check what other reviewers have flagged before writing yours.

Key files to review:
- `src/domain/next_action.rs` — domain types
- `src/infrastructure/next_action_repository.rs` — DB layer
- `src/services/next_action_service.rs` — service layer
- `src/routes/next_actions.rs` — route handlers + HTMX
- `templates/next_actions.html` — main page template
- `templates/next_action_item.html` — item template
- `templates/next_action_edit.html` — inline edit
- `static/css/main.css` — new CSS styles
- `docs/glossary.md` — updated glossary
- `tests/api/next_actions.rs` — integration tests
</teammate-message>

content.text: I'll start reviewing all the key files, beginning with the domain types and glossary since those are my primary focus.

content.content:
1→# Domain Glossary
     2→
     3→Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our GTD (Getting
     4→Things Done) application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business
     5→says "capture to inbox," the code says `capture_to_inbox()`.
     6→
     7→Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Core Domain Types
    12→
    13→| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
    14→|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
    15→| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
    16→| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
    17→| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
    18→| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
    19→| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
    20→| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
    21→| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
    22→| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
    23→| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |
    24→
    25→## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)
    26→
    27→| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
    28→|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
    29→| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
    30→| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
    31→| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
    32→| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
    33→| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
    34→| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
    35→| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
    36→| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
    37→| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
    38→| Edit a todo title | `update_todo_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists |
    39→
    40→## Domain Errors
    41→
    42→| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
    43→|-----------------|-----------|-------|
    44→| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
    45→| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
    46→| Todo not found (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
    47→| Not authorized (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
    48→| Todo not found (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
    49→| Not authorized (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
    50→| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
    51→| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
    52→| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |
    53→| Invalid title (add) | `AddTodoError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_todo service |
    54→| Todo not found (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
    55→| Not authorized (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
    56→| Invalid title (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_todo_title service |
    57→| Context name empty | `ContextNameError::Empty` | Context name cannot be blank |
    58→| Context name too long | `ContextNameError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Context name exceeds 50 character limit |
    59→| Invalid title (add next action) | `AddNextActionError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_next_action service |
    60→| Next action not found (complete) | `CompleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
    61→| Not authorized (complete next action) | `CompleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
    62→| Next action not found (delete) | `DeleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
    63→| Not authorized (delete next action) | `DeleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
    64→| Next action not found (edit) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
    65→| Not authorized (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
    66→| Invalid title (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_next_action_title service |
    67→
    68→## Error Copy Convention
    69→
    70→Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
    71→are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
    72→tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.
    73→
    74→The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
    75→call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.
    76→
    77→| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
    78→|-------------|-----------------|
    79→| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
    80→| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
    81→| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
    82→| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
    83→| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
    84→| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
    85→| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
    86→| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
    87→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
    88→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit) | "Title cannot be empty" |
    89→| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
    90→
    91→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add next action) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
    92→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit next action) | "Title cannot be empty" |
    93→| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (next action) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
    94→
    95→*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*
    96→
    97→---
    98→
    99→## Type Design Principles
   100→
   101→These principles govern how we model the domain:
   102→
   103→1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   104→   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   105→   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.
   106→
   107→2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   108→   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   109→   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   110→   inconsistent.
   111→
   112→3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   113→   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   114→   abstractions in Rust.
   115→
   116→4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   117→   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   118→   I/O has leaked into the domain.
   119→
   120→5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   121→   ```
   122→   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   123→   ```
   124→   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.
   125→
   126→## State Machine: TodoItem
   127→
   128→```
   129→                 add_todo()
   130→  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
   131→                                 │  ^
   132→               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
   133→                                 │  │
   134→                                 v  │
   135→                             Completed
   136→```
   137→
   138→Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
   139→The `update_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
   140→Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).
   141→
   142→---
   143→
   144→## GTD Domain Types (V1 -- Approved)
   145→
   146→*These types will replace the simple TodoItem model as we evolve to GTD. Approved by
   147→team consensus. Final type signatures subject to domain architect guidance during
   148→implementation.*
   149→
   150→### Core GTD Concepts
   151→
   152→| Domain Term | Proposed Rust Type | Notes |
   153→|-------------|-------------------|-------|
   154→| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
   155→| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
   156→| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
   157→| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. |
   158→| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
   159→| Context ID | `ContextId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a context. **Implemented.** |
   160→| Waiting For item | `WaitingForItem` | Delegated/blocked item. Has title, waiting_on (text), and date added. |
   161→| Someday/Maybe item | `SomedayMaybeItem` | Parked idea. Has title and created_at. Not committed to. |
   162→| Item title | `ItemTitle` | Replaces `TodoTitle`. Non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed. Same validation. |
   163→| Context name | `ContextName` | Non-empty, max 50 chars, trimmed. Validated at construction via `ContextName::parse()`. **Implemented.** |
   164→
   165→### GTD Actions (Ubiquitous Language)
   166→
   167→| Business Action | Proposed Rust Function / Method | Notes |
   168→|-----------------|-------------------------------|-------|
   169→| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
   170→| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context) |
   171→| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction |
   172→| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem |
   173→| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
   174→| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed |
   175→| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
   176→| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
   177→| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
   178→| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
   179→| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time |
   180→| Drop project | `Project::drop()` | Active -> Dropped; project abandoned |
   181→| Activate someday/maybe | `activate()` | SomedayMaybeItem -> InboxItem (for re-clarification) |
   182→| Resolve waiting for | `resolve()` | WaitingForItem -> completed or moved to inbox |
   183→| Add context | `add_context()` | Creates a new user-defined context |
   184→| Start weekly review | `start_review()` | Initiates the three-phase review flow |
   185→
   186→### GTD State Machines
   187→
   188→**InboxItem Lifecycle:**
   189→```
   190→                capture()
   191→  [nothing] ──────────────> InboxItem
   192→                                │
   193→                      clarify() │
   194→              ┌────────┬────────┼────────┬────────┐
   195→              v        v        v        v        v
   196→         NextAction  Project  WaitingFor  Someday  [trash]
   197→```
   198→
   199→**NextAction Lifecycle:**
   200→```
   201→                  clarify_as_next_action()
   202→  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────> NextAction (Active)
   203→                                               │
   204→                                   complete()  │
   205→                                               v
   206→                                         NextAction (Completed)
   207→```
   208→
   209→**Project Lifecycle:**
   210→```
   211→                    clarify_as_project()
   212→  InboxItem ──────────────────────────> Project (Active)
   213→                                           │  │
   214→                              complete()   │  │  drop()
   215→                                           v  v
   216→                                    Completed / Dropped
   217→```
   218→

file.content:
# Domain Glossary

Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our GTD (Getting
Things Done) application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business
says "capture to inbox," the code says `capture_to_inbox()`.

Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).

---

## Core Domain Types

| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |

## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
| Edit a todo title | `update_todo_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists |

## Domain Errors

| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
| Todo not found (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Todo not found (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |
| Invalid title (add) | `AddTodoError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_todo service |
| Todo not found (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Invalid title (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_todo_title service |
| Context name empty | `ContextNameError::Empty` | Context name cannot be blank |
| Context name too long | `ContextNameError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Context name exceeds 50 character limit |
| Invalid title (add next action) | `AddNextActionError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_next_action service |
| Next action not found (complete) | `CompleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
| Not authorized (complete next action) | `CompleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
| Next action not found (delete) | `DeleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete next action) | `DeleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
| Next action not found (edit) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
| Invalid title (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_next_action_title service |

## Error Copy Convention

Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.

The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.

| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |

| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add next action) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit next action) | "Title cannot be empty" |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (next action) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |

*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*

---

## Type Design Principles

These principles govern how we model the domain:

1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.

2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   inconsistent.

3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   abstractions in Rust.

4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   I/O has leaked into the domain.

5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   ```
   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   ```
   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.

## State Machine: TodoItem

```
                 add_todo()
  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
                                 │  ^
               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
                                 │  │
                                 v  │
                             Completed
```

Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
The `update_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).

---

## GTD Domain Types (V1 -- Approved)

*These types will replace the simple TodoItem model as we evolve to GTD. Approved by
team consensus. Final type signatures subject to domain architect guidance during
implementation.*

### Core GTD Concepts

| Domain Term | Proposed Rust Type | Notes |
|-------------|-------------------|-------|
| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. |
| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
| Context ID | `ContextId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a context. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting For item | `WaitingForItem` | Delegated/blocked item. Has title, waiting_on (text), and date added. |
| Someday/Maybe item | `SomedayMaybeItem` | Parked idea. Has title and created_at. Not committed to. |
| Item title | `ItemTitle` | Replaces `TodoTitle`. Non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed. Same validation. |
| Context name | `ContextName` | Non-empty, max 50 chars, trimmed. Validated at construction via `ContextName::parse()`. **Implemented.** |

### GTD Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Proposed Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|-------------------------------|-------|
| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context) |
| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction |
| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem |
| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed |
| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time |
| Drop project | `Project::drop()` | Active -> Dropped; project abandoned |
| Activate someday/maybe | `activate()` | SomedayMaybeItem -> InboxItem (for re-clarification) |
| Resolve waiting for | `resolve()` | WaitingForItem -> completed or moved to inbox |
| Add context | `add_context()` | Creates a new user-defined context |
| Start weekly review | `start_review()` | Initiates the three-phase review flow |

### GTD State Machines

**InboxItem Lifecycle:**
```
                capture()
  [nothing] ──────────────> InboxItem
                                │
                      clarify() │
              ┌────────┬────────┼────────┬────────┐
              v        v        v        v        v
         NextAction  Project  WaitingFor  Someday  [trash]
```

**NextAction Lifecycle:**
```
                  clarify_as_next_action()
  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────> NextAction (Active)
                                               │
                                   complete()  │
                                               v
                                         NextAction (Completed)
```

**Project Lifecycle:**
```
                    clarify_as_project()
  InboxItem ──────────────────────────> Project (Active)
                                           │  │
                              complete()   │  │  drop()
                                           v  v
                                    Completed / Dropped
```

content.text: Good. The domain type and glossary look solid. Now let me review the service layer, infrastructure, and routes.

content.content:
1→use sqlx::PgPool;
     2→
     3→use crate::domain::{ContextId, NextAction, NextActionId, TodoTitle, TodoTitleError, UserId};
     4→use crate::infrastructure::next_action_repository;
     5→
     6→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
     7→pub enum AddNextActionError {
     8→    #[error("Invalid title: {0}")]
     9→    InvalidTitle(#[from] TodoTitleError),
    10→    #[error(transparent)]
    11→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
    12→}
    13→
    14→#[tracing::instrument(
    15→    name = "add_next_action",
    16→    skip(pool, title),
    17→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid(), context_id = %context_id.as_uuid())
    18→)]
    19→pub async fn add_next_action(
    20→    pool: &PgPool,
    21→    user_id: UserId,
    22→    context_id: ContextId,
    23→    title: String,
    24→) -> Result<NextAction, AddNextActionError> {
    25→    let title = TodoTitle::parse(title)?;
    26→    let action = NextAction::new(user_id, context_id, title);
    27→
    28→    next_action_repository::insert_next_action(pool, &action)
    29→        .await
    30→        .map_err(|e| {
    31→            AddNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to insert next action: {e}"))
    32→        })?;
    33→
    34→    tracing::info!("Next action added");
    35→    Ok(action)
    36→}
    37→
    38→#[tracing::instrument(
    39→    name = "list_active_next_actions",
    40→    skip(pool),
    41→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    42→)]
    43→pub async fn list_active_next_actions(
    44→    pool: &PgPool,
    45→    user_id: &UserId,
    46→) -> Result<Vec<NextAction>, anyhow::Error> {
    47→    let actions = next_action_repository::find_active_next_actions_by_user(pool, user_id)
    48→        .await
    49→        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to fetch next actions: {e}"))?;
    50→
    51→    Ok(actions)
    52→}
    53→
    54→#[tracing::instrument(
    55→    name = "list_active_next_actions_by_context",
    56→    skip(pool),
    57→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid(), context_id = %context_id.as_uuid())
    58→)]
    59→pub async fn list_active_next_actions_by_context(
    60→    pool: &PgPool,
    61→    user_id: &UserId,
    62→    context_id: &ContextId,
    63→) -> Result<Vec<NextAction>, anyhow::Error> {
    64→    let actions = next_action_repository::find_active_next_actions_by_user_and_context(
    65→        pool, user_id, context_id,
    66→    )
    67→    .await
    68→    .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to fetch next actions by context: {e}"))?;
    69→
    70→    Ok(actions)
    71→}
    72→
    73→#[tracing::instrument(
    74→    name = "count_active_next_actions",
    75→    skip(pool),
    76→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    77→)]
    78→pub async fn count_active_next_actions(
    79→    pool: &PgPool,
    80→    user_id: &UserId,
    81→) -> Result<i64, anyhow::Error> {
    82→    let count = next_action_repository::count_active_next_actions(pool, user_id)
    83→        .await
    84→        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to count next actions: {e}"))?;
    85→
    86→    Ok(count)
    87→}
    88→
    89→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
    90→pub enum CompleteNextActionError {
    91→    #[error("Next action not found")]
    92→    NotFound,
    93→    #[error("Not authorized to complete this next action")]
    94→    Unauthorized,
    95→    #[error(transparent)]
    96→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
    97→}
    98→
    99→#[tracing::instrument(
   100→    name = "complete_next_action",
   101→    skip(pool),
   102→    fields(action_id = %action_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
   103→)]
   104→pub async fn complete_next_action(
   105→    pool: &PgPool,
   106→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   107→    user_id: &UserId,
   108→) -> Result<(), CompleteNextActionError> {
   109→    let action = next_action_repository::find_next_action_by_id(pool, action_id)
   110→        .await
   111→        .map_err(|e| CompleteNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
   112→        .ok_or(CompleteNextActionError::NotFound)?;
   113→
   114→    if action.user_id() != user_id {
   115→        return Err(CompleteNextActionError::Unauthorized);
   116→    }
   117→
   118→    let completed = action.complete();
   119→    if let NextAction::Completed { completed_at, .. } = &completed {
   120→        next_action_repository::complete_next_action(pool, action_id, completed_at)
   121→            .await
   122→            .map_err(|e| {
   123→                CompleteNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   124→                    "Failed to complete next action: {e}"
   125→                ))
   126→            })?;
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    tracing::info!("Next action completed");
   130→    Ok(())
   131→}
   132→
   133→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   134→pub enum DeleteNextActionError {
   135→    #[error("Next action not found")]
   136→    NotFound,
   137→    #[error("Not authorized to delete this next action")]
   138→    Unauthorized,
   139→    #[error(transparent)]
   140→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
   141→}
   142→
   143→#[tracing::instrument(
   144→    name = "delete_next_action",
   145→    skip(pool),
   146→    fields(action_id = %action_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
   147→)]
   148→pub async fn delete_next_action(
   149→    pool: &PgPool,
   150→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   151→    user_id: &UserId,
   152→) -> Result<(), DeleteNextActionError> {
   153→    let action = next_action_repository::find_next_action_by_id(pool, action_id)
   154→        .await
   155→        .map_err(|e| DeleteNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
   156→        .ok_or(DeleteNextActionError::NotFound)?;
   157→
   158→    if action.user_id() != user_id {
   159→        return Err(DeleteNextActionError::Unauthorized);
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    next_action_repository::delete_next_action(pool, action_id)
   163→        .await
   164→        .map_err(|e| {
   165→            DeleteNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to delete next action: {e}"))
   166→        })?;
   167→
   168→    tracing::info!("Next action deleted");
   169→    Ok(())
   170→}
   171→
   172→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   173→pub enum UpdateNextActionTitleError {
   174→    #[error("Next action not found")]
   175→    NotFound,
   176→    #[error("Not authorized to edit this next action")]
   177→    Unauthorized,
   178→    #[error("Invalid title: {0}")]
   179→    InvalidTitle(#[from] TodoTitleError),
   180→    #[error(transparent)]
   181→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
   182→}
   183→
   184→#[tracing::instrument(
   185→    name = "update_next_action_title",
   186→    skip(pool, new_title),
   187→    fields(action_id = %action_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
   188→)]
   189→pub async fn update_next_action_title(
   190→    pool: &PgPool,
   191→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   192→    user_id: &UserId,
   193→    new_title: String,
   194→) -> Result<(), UpdateNextActionTitleError> {
   195→    let action = next_action_repository::find_next_action_by_id(pool, action_id)
   196→        .await
   197→        .map_err(|e| {
   198→            UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}"))
   199→        })?
   200→        .ok_or(UpdateNextActionTitleError::NotFound)?;
   201→
   202→    if action.user_id() != user_id {
   203→        return Err(UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unauthorized);
   204→    }
   205→
   206→    let title = TodoTitle::parse(new_title)?;
   207→
   208→    next_action_repository::update_next_action_title(pool, action_id, &title)
   209→        .await
   210→        .map_err(|e| {
   211→            UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   212→                "Failed to update next action title: {e}"
   213→            ))
   214→        })?;
   215→
   216→    tracing::info!("Next action title updated");
   217→    Ok(())
   218→}
   219→

1→use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
     2→use sqlx::PgPool;
     3→use uuid::Uuid;
     4→
     5→use crate::domain::{ContextId, NextAction, NextActionId, TodoTitle, UserId};
     6→
     7→struct NextActionRecord {
     8→    id: Uuid,
     9→    user_id: Uuid,
    10→    context_id: Uuid,
    11→    title: String,
    12→    created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    13→    completed_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    14→}
    15→
    16→impl NextActionRecord {
    17→    fn into_domain(self) -> NextAction {
    18→        let id = NextActionId::from_uuid(self.id);
    19→        let user_id = UserId::from_uuid(self.user_id);
    20→        let context_id = ContextId::from_uuid(self.context_id);
    21→        let title = TodoTitle::parse(self.title).expect("stored title should be valid");
    22→
    23→        NextAction::from_parts(
    24→            id,
    25→            user_id,
    26→            context_id,
    27→            title,
    28→            self.created_at,
    29→            self.completed_at,
    30→        )
    31→    }
    32→}
    33→
    34→pub async fn insert_next_action(pool: &PgPool, action: &NextAction) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
    35→    let completed_at: Option<&DateTime<Utc>> = match action {
    36→        NextAction::Completed { completed_at, .. } => Some(completed_at),
    37→        NextAction::Active { .. } => None,
    38→    };
    39→
    40→    sqlx::query!(
    41→        r#"INSERT INTO next_actions (id, user_id, context_id, title, created_at, completed_at)
    42→           VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5 :: timestamptz, $6 :: timestamptz)"#,
    43→        action.id().as_uuid(),
    44→        action.user_id().as_uuid(),
    45→        action.context_id().as_uuid(),
    46→        action.title().as_str(),
    47→        action.created_at() as &DateTime<Utc>,
    48→        completed_at as Option<&DateTime<Utc>>,
    49→    )
    50→    .execute(pool)
    51→    .await?;
    52→    Ok(())
    53→}
    54→
    55→pub async fn find_active_next_actions_by_user(
    56→    pool: &PgPool,
    57→    user_id: &UserId,
    58→) -> Result<Vec<NextAction>, sqlx::Error> {
    59→    let records = sqlx::query_as!(
    60→        NextActionRecord,
    61→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, context_id, title,
    62→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>",
    63→           completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    64→           FROM next_actions
    65→           WHERE user_id = $1 AND completed_at IS NULL
    66→           ORDER BY created_at ASC"#,
    67→        user_id.as_uuid(),
    68→    )
    69→    .fetch_all(pool)
    70→    .await?;
    71→
    72→    Ok(records
    73→        .into_iter()
    74→        .map(|r: NextActionRecord| r.into_domain())
    75→        .collect())
    76→}
    77→
    78→pub async fn find_active_next_actions_by_user_and_context(
    79→    pool: &PgPool,
    80→    user_id: &UserId,
    81→    context_id: &ContextId,
    82→) -> Result<Vec<NextAction>, sqlx::Error> {
    83→    let records = sqlx::query_as!(
    84→        NextActionRecord,
    85→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, context_id, title,
    86→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>",
    87→           completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    88→           FROM next_actions
    89→           WHERE user_id = $1 AND context_id = $2 AND completed_at IS NULL
    90→           ORDER BY created_at ASC"#,
    91→        user_id.as_uuid(),
    92→        context_id.as_uuid(),
    93→    )
    94→    .fetch_all(pool)
    95→    .await?;
    96→
    97→    Ok(records
    98→        .into_iter()
    99→        .map(|r: NextActionRecord| r.into_domain())
   100→        .collect())
   101→}
   102→
   103→pub async fn find_next_action_by_id(
   104→    pool: &PgPool,
   105→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   106→) -> Result<Option<NextAction>, sqlx::Error> {
   107→    let record: Option<NextActionRecord> = sqlx::query_as!(
   108→        NextActionRecord,
   109→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, context_id, title,
   110→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>",
   111→           completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime<Utc>"
   112→           FROM next_actions WHERE id = $1"#,
   113→        action_id.as_uuid(),
   114→    )
   115→    .fetch_optional(pool)
   116→    .await?;
   117→
   118→    Ok(record.map(|r: NextActionRecord| r.into_domain()))
   119→}
   120→
   121→pub async fn complete_next_action(
   122→    pool: &PgPool,
   123→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   124→    completed_at: &DateTime<Utc>,
   125→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   126→    sqlx::query!(
   127→        r#"UPDATE next_actions SET completed_at = $1 :: timestamptz WHERE id = $2"#,
   128→        completed_at as &DateTime<Utc>,
   129→        action_id.as_uuid(),
   130→    )
   131→    .execute(pool)
   132→    .await?;
   133→    Ok(())
   134→}
   135→
   136→pub async fn delete_next_action(
   137→    pool: &PgPool,
   138→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   139→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   140→    sqlx::query!(
   141→        r#"DELETE FROM next_actions WHERE id = $1"#,
   142→        action_id.as_uuid(),
   143→    )
   144→    .execute(pool)
   145→    .await?;
   146→    Ok(())
   147→}
   148→
   149→pub async fn update_next_action_title(
   150→    pool: &PgPool,
   151→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   152→    title: &TodoTitle,
   153→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   154→    sqlx::query!(
   155→        r#"UPDATE next_actions SET title = $1 WHERE id = $2"#,
   156→        title.as_str(),
   157→        action_id.as_uuid(),
   158→    )
   159→    .execute(pool)
   160→    .await?;
   161→    Ok(())
   162→}
   163→
   164→pub async fn count_active_next_actions(
   165→    pool: &PgPool,
   166→    user_id: &UserId,
   167→) -> Result<i64, sqlx::Error> {
   168→    let record = sqlx::query!(
   169→        r#"SELECT COUNT(*) as "count!" FROM next_actions WHERE user_id = $1 AND completed_at IS NULL"#,
   170→        user_id.as_uuid(),
   171→    )
   172→    .fetch_one(pool)
   173→    .await?;
   174→
   175→    Ok(record.count)
   176→}
   177→

content.text: Now let me check the integration tests to see if the domain behavior is well-covered.
I've now done a thorough review of all the key files. Let me send my review to Luca.

input.content:
## Domain Architecture Review -- Step 4: Next Actions with Contexts

Excellent work, Luca. The implementation is clean and follows our established patterns faithfully. Here is my detailed review:

---

### What is done well

1. **NextAction as an enum state machine** (`src/domain/next_action.rs:40-56`): The Active/Completed enum pattern is exactly right. It mirrors the TodoItem pattern and makes illegal states unrepresentable -- no boolean `is_completed` with a nullable `completed_at` that could be inconsistent. The `complete()` method (`line 136-154`) correctly consumes `self` and returns the new state.

2. **Mandatory ContextId**: A NextAction MUST have a `context_id: ContextId` in both variants. This is correct GTD modeling -- a next action without a context is meaningless. The type system enforces this constraint at construction time.

3. **Proper newtypes**: `NextActionId(Uuid)` follows the established pattern with `new()`, `from_uuid()`, `as_uuid()`, and `Default`. Consistent with `InboxItemId`, `TodoItemId`, `ContextId`.

4. **Pure domain core**: No I/O in `src/domain/next_action.rs`. Only imports are `chrono`, `uuid`, and sibling domain types. The domain layer remains trivially testable.

5. **`from_parts()` reconstruction** (`line 69-94`): Using `Option<DateTime<Utc>>` to discriminate Active vs Completed when reconstructing from the DB is the right approach -- it matches how the data is stored (nullable `completed_at`) while producing the correct sum type.

6. **Service error types**: `CompleteNextActionError`, `DeleteNextActionError`, `UpdateNextActionTitleError` all follow the established pattern with `NotFound`, `Unauthorized`, and `Unexpected` variants. Domain errors and infrastructure errors are properly separated.

7. **Glossary updated**: `docs/glossary.md` now includes NextAction, NextActionId, and all the new service-level actions and error types, with **Implemented** markers on the GTD Concepts table. Well done.

8. **DB migration**: The `next_actions` table correctly has `context_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES contexts(id)` -- the NOT NULL + FK constraint mirrors the domain type's mandatory context. Also includes `project_id UUID NULL` for future Step 6, which is forward-thinking but appropriate since the column needs to exist in the schema.

9. **Unit tests** (9 tests in `next_action.rs`): Good coverage of construction, state transitions, and `from_parts` reconstruction. Test names read as behavior specifications.

10. **Integration tests**: 19 tests covering page loading, adding, completing, deleting, editing, context filtering, user isolation, HTMX fragments, and unauthenticated access. Thorough.

---

### Issues

**No BLOCKING issues found.**

**Minor:**

1. **Title type reuse** (Minor): The glossary originally proposed `ItemTitle` to replace `TodoTitle` as a shared title type across all GTD entities (InboxItem, NextAction, etc.). Currently NextAction reuses `TodoTitle` directly, which works fine but means the name in code (`TodoTitle`) doesn't match the ubiquitous language for a NextAction title. This is acceptable for now since the validation logic is identical and renaming carries refactoring risk, but we should track a rename to `ItemTitle` (or similar) in `docs/deferred-items.md` to align the code with the glossary during the cleanup step (Step 13).

2. **`get_edit_next_action` bypasses the service layer** (Minor, `src/routes/next_actions.rs:298-302`): The GET edit handler calls `next_action_repository::find_next_action_by_id` directly instead of going through the service layer. The other handlers (complete, delete, update title) all go through services. This is a small consistency gap. Not blocking since the handler still does the ownership check, but the service layer is where we expect that orchestration to live.

**Nice-to-have:**

1. **Missing `completed_at()` accessor**: The `NextAction` type has accessors for `id()`, `user_id()`, `context_id()`, `title()`, `created_at()`, but no `completed_at()` that returns `Option<&DateTime<Utc>>` (or is only available on the Completed variant). The service layer currently pattern-matches on the enum to extract it (`next_action_service.rs:119`), which is fine, but a `completed_at()` accessor would be consistent with the other accessors. Not needed now but will become useful when we show completed actions in project views (Step 6).

2. **`get_next_action_item` route** (`routes/next_actions.rs:392-421`): Same observation as Minor #2 -- this also bypasses the service layer and calls the repository directly. Both this and the edit GET handler could use a `get_next_action()` service function for consistency.

---

### Consensus

From a domain architecture perspective, this implementation is **approved**. The NextAction type correctly models the GTD domain concept, the enum state machine enforces valid transitions, illegal states are unrepresentable, the domain core is pure, and the glossary is updated. The minor items can be tracked as deferred work.

Well done, team.
input.summary: Domain review: approved, no blocking issues

4. **Pure domain core**: No I/O in `src/domain/next_action.rs`. Only imports are `chrono`, `uuid`, and sibling domain types. The domain layer remains trivially testable.

5. **`from_parts()` reconstruction** (`line 69-94`): Using `Option<DateTime<Utc>>` to discriminate Active vs Completed when reconstructing from the DB is the right approach -- it matches how the data is stored (nullable `completed_at`) while producing the correct sum type.

6. **Service error types**: `CompleteNextActionError`, `DeleteNextActionError`, `UpdateNextActionTitleError` all follow the established pattern with `NotFound`, `Unauthorized`, and `Unexpected` variants. Domain errors and infrastructure errors are properly separated.

7. **Glossary updated**: `docs/glossary.md` now includes NextAction, NextActionId, and all the new service-level actions and error types, with **Implemented** markers on the GTD Concepts table. Well done.

8. **DB migration**: The `next_actions` table correctly has `context_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES contexts(id)` -- the NOT NULL + FK constraint mirrors the domain type's mandatory context. Also includes `project_id UUID NULL` for future Step 6, which is forward-thinking but appropriate since the column needs to exist in the schema.

9. **Unit tests** (9 tests in `next_action.rs`): Good coverage of construction, state transitions, and `from_parts` reconstruction. Test names read as behavior specifications.

10. **Integration tests**: 19 tests covering page loading, adding, completing, deleting, editing, context filtering, user isolation, HTMX fragments, and unauthenticated access. Thorough.

---

### Issues

**No BLOCKING issues found.**

**Minor:**

1. **Title type reuse** (Minor): The glossary originally proposed `ItemTitle` to replace `TodoTitle` as a shared title type across all GTD entities (InboxItem, NextAction, etc.). Currently NextAction reuses `TodoTitle` directly, which works fine but means the name in code (`TodoTitle`) doesn't match the ubiquitous language for a NextAction title. This is acceptable for now since the validation logic is identical and renaming carries refactoring risk, but we should track a rename to `ItemTitle` (or similar) in `docs/deferred-items.md` to align the code with the glossary during the cleanup step (Step 13).

2. **`get_edit_next_action` bypasses the service layer** (Minor, `src/routes/next_actions.rs:298-302`): The GET edit handler calls `next_action_repository::find_next_action_by_id` directly instead of going through the service layer. The other handlers (complete, delete, update title) all go through services. This is a small consistency gap. Not blocking since the handler still does the ownership check, but the service layer is where we expect that orchestration to live.

**Nice-to-have:**

1. **Missing `completed_at()` accessor**: The `NextAction` type has accessors for `id()`, `user_id()`, `context_id()`, `title()`, `created_at()`, but no `completed_at()` that returns `Option<&DateTime<Utc>>` (or is only available on the Completed variant). The service layer currently pattern-matches on the enum to extract it (`next_action_service.rs:119`), which is fine, but a `completed_at()` accessor would be consistent with the other accessors. Not needed now but will become useful when we show completed actions in project views (Step 6).

2. **`get_next_action_item` route** (`routes/next_actions.rs:392-421`): Same observation as Minor #2 -- this also bypasses the service layer and calls the repository directly. Both this and the edit GET handler could use a `get_next_action()` service function for consistency.

---

### Consensus

From a domain architecture perspective, this implementation is **approved**. The NextAction type correctly models the GTD domain concept, the enum state machine enforces valid transitions, illegal states are unrepresentable, the domain core is pure, and the glossary is updated. The minor items can be tracked as deferred work.

Well done, team."
  }
}

routing.summary: Domain review: approved, no blocking issues
routing.content:
## Domain Architecture Review -- Step 4: Next Actions with Contexts

Excellent work, Luca. The implementation is clean and follows our established patterns faithfully. Here is my detailed review:

---

### What is done well

1. **NextAction as an enum state machine** (`src/domain/next_action.rs:40-56`): The Active/Completed enum pattern is exactly right. It mirrors the TodoItem pattern and makes illegal states unrepresentable -- no boolean `is_completed` with a nullable `completed_at` that could be inconsistent. The `complete()` method (`line 136-154`) correctly consumes `self` and returns the new state.

2. **Mandatory ContextId**: A NextAction MUST have a `context_id: ContextId` in both variants. This is correct GTD modeling -- a next action without a context is meaningless. The type system enforces this constraint at construction time.

3. **Proper newtypes**: `NextActionId(Uuid)` follows the established pattern with `new()`, `from_uuid()`, `as_uuid()`, and `Default`. Consistent with `InboxItemId`, `TodoItemId`, `ContextId`.

4. **Pure domain core**: No I/O in `src/domain/next_action.rs`. Only imports are `chrono`, `uuid`, and sibling domain types. The domain layer remains trivially testable.

5. **`from_parts()` reconstruction** (`line 69-94`): Using `Option<DateTime<Utc>>` to discriminate Active vs Completed when reconstructing from the DB is the right approach -- it matches how the data is stored (nullable `completed_at`) while producing the correct sum type.

6. **Service error types**: `CompleteNextActionError`, `DeleteNextActionError`, `UpdateNextActionTitleError` all follow the established pattern with `NotFound`, `Unauthorized`, and `Unexpected` variants. Domain errors and infrastructure errors are properly separated.

7. **Glossary updated**: `docs/glossary.md` now includes NextAction, NextActionId, and all the new service-level actions and error types, with **Implemented** markers on the GTD Concepts table. Well done.

8. **DB migration**: The `next_actions` table correctly has `context_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES contexts(id)` -- the NOT NULL + FK constraint mirrors the domain type's mandatory context. Also includes `project_id UUID NULL` for future Step 6, which is forward-thinking but appropriate since the column needs to exist in the schema.

9. **Unit tests** (9 tests in `next_action.rs`): Good coverage of construction, state transitions, and `from_parts` reconstruction. Test names read as behavior specifications.

10. **Integration tests**: 19 tests covering page loading, adding, completing, deleting, editing, context filtering, user isolation, HTMX fragments, and unauthenticated access. Thorough.

---

### Issues

**No BLOCKING issues found.**

**Minor:**

1. **Title type reuse** (Minor): The glossary originally proposed `ItemTitle` to replace `TodoTitle` as a shared title type across all GTD entities (InboxItem, NextAction, etc.). Currently NextAction reuses `TodoTitle` directly, which works fine but means the name in code (`TodoTitle`) doesn't match the ubiquitous language for a NextAction title. This is acceptable for now since the validation logic is identical and renaming carries refactoring risk, but we should track a rename to `ItemTitle` (or similar) in `docs/deferred-items.md` to align the code with the glossary during the cleanup step (Step 13).

2. **`get_edit_next_action` bypasses the service layer** (Minor, `src/routes/next_actions.rs:298-302`): The GET edit handler calls `next_action_repository::find_next_action_by_id` directly instead of going through the service layer. The other handlers (complete, delete, update title) all go through services. This is a small consistency gap. Not blocking since the handler still does the ownership check, but the service layer is where we expect that orchestration to live.

**Nice-to-have:**

1. **Missing `completed_at()` accessor**: The `NextAction` type has accessors for `id()`, `user_id()`, `context_id()`, `title()`, `created_at()`, but no `completed_at()` that returns `Option<&DateTime<Utc>>` (or is only available on the Completed variant). The service layer currently pattern-matches on the enum to extract it (`next_action_service.rs:119`), which is fine, but a `completed_at()` accessor would be consistent with the other accessors. Not needed now but will become useful when we show completed actions in project views (Step 6).

2. **`get_next_action_item` route** (`routes/next_actions.rs:392-421`): Same observation as Minor #2 -- this also bypasses the service layer and calls the repository directly. Both this and the edit GET handler could use a `get_next_action()` service function for consistency.

---

### Consensus

From a domain architecture perspective, this implementation is **approved**. The NextAction type correctly models the GTD domain concept, the enum state machine enforces valid transitions, illegal states are unrepresentable, the domain core is pure, and the glossary is updated. The minor items can be tracked as deferred work.

Well done, team.

content.text:
I've completed my domain architecture review and sent it to Luca. Here's a summary of my findings:

**No BLOCKING issues.** The implementation is approved from a domain architecture perspective.

Key strengths:
- `NextAction` enum (Active/Completed) correctly models the GTD state machine
- Mandatory `ContextId` enforced by the type system
- Pure domain core with no I/O leaking in
- Proper newtypes following established patterns
- Glossary properly updated with all new types and actions

Two **Minor** items (not blocking):
1. `TodoTitle` reuse -- the glossary proposed `ItemTitle` as a shared type; we should track the rename for Step 13
2. Two route handlers bypass the service layer and call the repository directly

Two **Nice-to-have** items:
1. Missing `completed_at()` accessor on `NextAction`
2. A `get_next_action()` service function would improve consistency

I'm waiting for the rest of the team's reviews and ready to give final consensus.
